_meta.description: Live macro themes affecting currencies but not on the scheduled news calendar. Update as regime-changing events happen. Scanner reads this at brief generation time and displays it in the daily/weekly review.
_meta.howToUse: Each theme should include: (1) short title, (2) start date, (3) currency implications with brief reasoning, (4) a specific watchLevel that would invalidate the theme, (5) affectedPairs for scanner correlation. Retire themes by moving to _retired[] with an endDate.
_meta.maintenance: Manual updates + Claude WebSearch each session. 2026-07-09: Iran/oil theme reactivated after Trump declared ceasefire 'over'; oil spiked $75.7 intraday. Fed pivot theme softened — hawkish June minutes but DXY still weakened.

themes.title: Iran ceasefire declared over — oil back to $73-75 range
themes.summary: Trump declared temporary US-Iran ceasefire 'over.' WTI crude spiked toward $75.7 intraday on Jul 8 before easing to ~$73.8 on Strait of Hormuz + reciprocal-strike risk. CAD tailwind is RE-ACTIVATED after briefly retracing. Watch $70 as prior low — theme dies below $70 sustained.

implications.CAD: tailwind reactivated — oil bid back on
implications.USD: mixed — haven bid vs Fed pivot fatigue (DXY weakened even on hawkish minutes)
implications.AUD: mixed — risk-off drag vs commodity currency lift

themes.watchLevel: CL1! sustained below $70 = CAD-strength commodity leg gone; $77+ = escalation extending

themes.title: Fed's hawkish June minutes met with USD weakness — pivot may be priced in
themes.summary: FOMC held rates at 3.5-3.75%. June meeting minutes showed a few officials wanted to hike, which should have supported USD but DXY weakened against every G10 except JPY. Signal: the data-dependent hawkish stance is already priced. Additional hawkish surprises now needed to move DXY up. Watch CPI Tue.

implications.USD: softening tailwind — hawkish news losing bite
implications.CAD: less headwind than the pure Warsh theme implied
implications.GBP: tailwind — bounced hardest against USD weakness
implications.JPY: headwind persisting — carry gap keeps yen weakest of G10

themes.watchLevel: DXY closes below 100 = pivot theme officially over; CPI Tue is the resolver

themes.title: USMCA review + Supreme Court tariff ruling create headline risk on CAD/USD
themes.summary: USMCA agreement review is in progress. Supreme Court expected to rule on Reciprocal Tariff executive orders. Either announcement can hit CAD/MXN/USD sharply and unpredictably.

implications.CAD: elevated vol both directions — soften position sizes
implications.MXN: highest exposure but not in your book

themes.watchLevel: USMCA outcome OR Supreme Court ruling released = theme resolves

_retired.title: US-Iran conflict jump largely retraced — oil hovering near $70-74
_retired.endReason: Reactivated as new theme after Trump declared ceasefire over on Jul 8; oil back to $73-75 range
